what_it_does: Implements a browser localStorage-backed store (window.MS_FEEDBACK) for raw user feedback and curated AI learnings, plus a React hook (useFeedbackStore). It persists feedback entries, operator-curated learnings, and session records under a single key, composes scope-aware prompt text from active learnings, tracks whether each learning was actually injected in the current session, and exposes machine-readable prefer/avoid signals for a resolver.
observations: Uses a single localStorage key 'ms_feedback_v1' for feedback, learnings, and sessions.
Generates a random per-reload session id and records session start in state.sessions (capped at 50) but deliberately skips persisting during registration.
Distinguishes read-only access (learningsForScope, signalsFor, learningsFor) from prompt-injection access (learningsForPrompt) which marks learnings as applied.
Tracks application provenance via appliedSessions, appliedCount, and lastAppliedAt to show whether a memory 'fired'.
Provides promoteFeedback to turn a raw feedback entry into an active learning using a small action-to-kind mapping.
Exposes signalsFor as a machine-readable {prefer, avoid} list intended for MS_RESOLVE bias.
Includes a React useFeedbackStore hook that subscribes to all store changes and returns the whole state object.
Active learnings match a requested scope or the 'global' scope.
Uses prefixed random ids (fb_, ln_, s_) and defensive try/catch around localStorage.
The byKind map converts learning kinds into imperative prompt headings.
standouts: The appliedSessions/appliedCount mechanism is the core 'did it actually fire' proof.
signalsFor separates machine-readable resolver bias from human-readable prompt text.
promoteFeedback bridges raw feedback into curated, behavior-changing learnings.
conventions: JSON serialization to localStorage with fallback empty state.
Pub/sub pattern via a Set of subscriber callbacks.
Defensive try/catch around storage and subscriber calls.
Functional state object mutated in place rather than immutable updates.
concerns: registerSession mutates state.sessions but does not call persist(), so session records can be lost if no other write occurs before reload.
appliedCount increments every time a learning is composed into a prompt, even multiple times in the same session, so it measures inclusions rather than distinct sessions.
No validation of entry/learning shapes or required fields.
useFeedbackStore returns the entire state object, which may cause unnecessary React re-renders on unrelated changes.
The localStorage key is global across projects, risking collisions if multiple apps share the same origin.
Random id generation has a non-zero collision probability and no fallback check.
notes: A local-first, session-aware feedback and learning store whose main trick is proving whether a curated instruction actually reached the AI prompt.
questions: What is the exact expected shape of an entry passed to addFeedback?
What are the valid scope values besides 'intake' and 'global'?
How does MS_RESOLVE consume signalsFor and how are prefer/avoid signals weighted?
Which UI component calls promoteFeedback and what overrides does it supply?
How does the Memory subsystem read state.sessions for 'last seen' history?
Is React guaranteed to be globally available wherever useFeedbackStore is used?
What is the full structure of targetRef and its contextHint field?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual implementation: it stores feedback and learnings, composes prompt instructions, and tracks application to verify memory firing.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give operators a durable way to teach the AI preferences, ensure those teachings are injected into prompts, and provide evidence that they were actually used during a session.
novelty: The separation between read-only learning access and prompt-injection access that mutates application provenance, plus the explicit machine-readable signalsFor bias channel alongside human-readable prompt text.
contribution: A reusable, scope-aware feedback and learning persistence layer with prompt composition, application tracking, and resolver bias signals that a fused system could keep as its memory/feedback substrate.
summary_for_embedding: feedback-store.js is a browser localStorage-backed store for user feedback and curated AI learnings that composes scope-aware prompt instructions and tracks whether each learning was injected in the current session.
intention_for_embedding: To let operators capture feedback, promote it into active instructions, inject those instructions into AI prompts, and verify they actually affected generation.

symbols.does: Reads the persisted store object from localStorage or returns a default empty state.
symbols.description: Parses the JSON stored under 'ms_feedback_v1' and ensures feedback, learnings, and sessions arrays exist; returns a default empty structure on any error.

symbols.does: Writes the current state to localStorage and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Serializes state to localStorage under the fixed key, then calls every subscriber function with the current state, swallowing errors.

symbols.does: Registers a callback to receive state updates and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: Adds a subscriber function to an internal Set; the returned function removes it, enabling reactive consumers such as the React hook.

symbols.does: Returns the current in-memory state object.
symbols.description: Exposes the live state reference so callers can inspect feedback, learnings, sessions, and metadata directly.

symbols.does: Appends a raw user feedback entry to state and persists.
symbols.description: Creates a feedback item with a generated id, timestamp, current session id, and 'submitted' status, prepends it to state.feedback, persists, and returns the item.

symbols.does: Adds a new curated learning that can later be injected into prompts.
symbols.description: Creates an active learning with default scope 'intake' and kind 'prefer', initializes application counters, prepends it to state.learnings, persists, and returns the item.

symbols.does: Toggles the active flag of a learning by id.
symbols.description: Finds the learning with the given id and sets its active boolean, persisting the change so it is included or excluded from future prompts.

symbols.does: Deletes a learning from state by id.
symbols.description: Filters the learnings array to remove the specified learning and persists the updated state.

symbols.does: Merges a patch object into an existing learning.
symbols.description: Finds the learning by id and applies Object.assign with the provided patch, then persists.

symbols.does: Returns composed prompt text for active learnings and marks them as applied this session.
symbols.description: Calls _composeLearnings with mark=true, so every included learning records the current session in appliedSessions, increments appliedCount, and updates lastAppliedAt.

symbols.does: Returns composed prompt text without marking learnings as applied.
symbols.description: Calls _composeLearnings with mark=false, intended for previewing prompt content without falsifying the 'memory fired' proof.

symbols.does: Builds a formatted 'LEARNED PREFERENCES' prompt block from active learnings.
symbols.description: Filters active learnings by scope (including global), maps each kind to an imperative heading, optionally marks them applied, and joins them into a single prompt string.

symbols.does: Counts active learnings matching a scope.
symbols.description: Returns the number of active learnings whose scope matches the requested scope or is 'global'.

symbols.does: Returns machine-readable prefer/avoid strings for a scope.
symbols.description: Collects active 'prefer', 'should_show', and 'avoid' learning texts into {prefer, avoid} arrays for resolver bias without marking application.

symbols.does: Lists active learnings matching a scope and optional label substring.
symbols.description: Filters active learnings by scope and, if provided, by whether the learning text or context contains the label; read-only and does not mark applied.

symbols.does: Checks whether a learning was applied in the current session.
symbols.description: Returns true if the learning's appliedSessions array exists and contains the current session id.

symbols.does: Converts a raw feedback entry into an active learning.
symbols.description: Looks up the feedback by id, maps its action to a learning kind (or uses overrides), creates a learning via addLearning, links the feedback's promotedTo field, persists, and returns the learning.

symbols.does: Deletes a feedback entry from state by id.
symbols.description: Filters state.feedback to remove the specified entry and persists the updated state.
